change_summary: detail_evidence shrank from 12,877 to 4,339 chars (-66.3%) via commit 66c9a741 (2026-05-16) — operator-direct EXP-401 compression (1888w -> 528w, 3.58x) integrated without curmudgeon-in-the-loop per operator authorization. Most lost arguments relocated to Part 6.14 (the kappa Coupling Cluster) and WIN-070 (Forensic Zoom forensics). Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-19T01:10Z, detail_evidence_length field; obe_skipped=0 (WIN-012 was head of list and not yet re-reviewed since 2026-04-21 c6).
our_argument_summary: WIN-012 attacks kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal as self-contradicted: (a) WIN-013/014 eclipse-gravity nulls void kappa's denominator; (b) the numerator is a single storm/eclipse event with no demonstrated broad applicability; (c) numerator and denominator come from different events at different sites with different instruments — no co-located measurement exists; (d) WIN-011's thermal-tilt-artifact concession destroys kappa even granting the number; (e) all defender-rescues destroy PRED-TIER-3 by stripping its derivation. Post-EXP-401 the heavy structural argument is consolidated into Part 6.14; WIN-012 retains the high-level vise and delegates the quantitative closure.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (lines 86-87: WIN-012 — Magnetic-Gravity Coupling Constant CONFIRMED)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (lines 224-226: Domain EM-Gravity Coupling kappa, Source: BOU 2017 eclipse)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dome page reports kappa = 10.9 nT / 6.5 uGal = 1.677 nT/uGal. Verified arithmetic: 10.9/6.5 = 1.6769 (rounds to 1.68 at 2dp; dome quotes 1.67). Forward chain: PRED-TIER-1 dB = -17 to -21 nT yields dg = 10.14 to 12.52 uGal via dB/kappa — matches PRED-TIER-3 target 10-13 uGal. Mohe denominator self-consistent by construction.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. Live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile (per raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 225) shows Predicted 1.67, Observed 1.677, Tolerance 5%, Status PASS — the 'observed' value is the same 10.9/6.5 ratio that defines kappa. No external API fetch.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 1999/2009 SG nulls rescued via (a) 'campaigns not optimized for C1/C4 timing'; (b) 'Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release'; (c) dielectric-boundary-transition mechanism reframe. All three closed in Part 6.14.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None within WIN-012 detail_evidence. But see internal cross-page event-provenance conflict below (Hole 2).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile: Predicted 1.67, Observed 1.677, Tolerance 5%, Status PASS. The 'observed' value 1.677 = 10.9/6.5, recomputing the same hardcoded ratio that defined kappa. No live data fetch; no comparison against any independent measurement.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome relabels (i) the Chapman Sq-dynamo ionospheric current explanation of eclipse magnetic anomalies and (ii) the thermal-tilt-artifact / shielding interpretations of the Mohe gravity signal as a unified 'magnetic-gravity coupling.' The dielectric-boundary-transition reframing of WIN-011 is itself a relabel of contact-phase ionospheric/instrumental effects. No new physical mechanism is derived.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: kappa = 1.67 was computed post-hoc from two pre-existing published observations (Mohe 1997, published Wang et al. 2000; BOU 2017 eclipse per dome live-power, or BOU 2003 Halloween storm per WIN-012 detail — see Hole 2). Both observations predate the kappa definition. No pre-registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: kappa is a ratio of two independently measured field values; it cannot be derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome-geometric parameter. The dielectric-boundary-transition reframing retains the number while abandoning the mechanism, demonstrating that kappa is parameter-fit residue, not a geometric derivation.

holes_found.description: TLDR factual error. tldr_evidence currently reads: 'The 1.67 nT/uGal coupling constant comes from dividing a disputed gravity reading by a magnetic measurement — both from the same unreplicated 1997 experiment.' This is wrong by both the detail_evidence and the dome's own source attribution. Per the detail, the numerator is BOU (Boulder, Colorado) and the denominator is Mohe (China) — two different sites, different instruments, different events. Per the dome's raw-text/01-home.txt line 32 and raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 226, the numerator is from 'BOU 2017 eclipse,' not 1997. The 1997 experiment is the Mohe denominator only. The TLDR collapses two distinct events into one, making it factually wrong. Per curmudgeon prompt: 'TLDR factual errors are major (readers see TLDRs first and may not expand).' This error has been live since the TLDR rollout on 2026-04-12 (commit fcd38da5) and survived c2-c6 reviews undetected — c6 hole #3 flagged TLDR scope but missed the factual error.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite tldr_evidence to correctly identify the two sources. Suggested: 'The 1.67 nT/uGal coupling constant divides a magnetic anomaly measured at Boulder, Colorado by a gravity anomaly measured at Mohe, China — two different sites, different instruments, different events. The Mohe gravity result has not been independently replicated; later superconducting gravimeter measurements at multiple sites found zero gravity signal, which would make this ratio undefined.' (Note: settling the BOU-2017-eclipse vs BOU-2003-Halloween-storm question — see Hole 2 — is a prerequisite to writing accurate provenance into the TLDR.)

holes_found.description: Cross-page inconsistency on kappa-numerator event provenance. Compressed WIN-012 detail_evidence opens with '-10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, October 2003 G5 Halloween storm, Dst = -383 nT).' But Part 6.14 (sections.json:part6.html, #p6-614-kappa-cluster) opens with '10.9 nT (BOU magnetometer, 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage)' — and the dome's own live-power page (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 226) labels the kappa-source as 'BOU 2017 eclipse.' These are different events six years apart. The dome itself appears internally inconsistent (its Forensic Zoom chart on the home page shows the 2003 Halloween storm timestamp 00:55-01:22 UTC per WIN-070 detail; its live-power tile attributes the same kappa value to BOU 2017 eclipse). Our review needs a single canonical attribution. Pre-existing — both c5/c6 also said '2003 Halloween storm' — so this hole pre-dates the compression. But the compression preserves the conflict.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) revise WIN-012 detail_evidence opening paragraph to match Part 6.14 ('BOU 2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage') and surface the dome's internal inconsistency (the -10.9 nT number is labeled 'BOU 2017 eclipse' on live-power but plotted as the 2003 Halloween storm in the home-page Forensic Zoom chart) — this is rhetorically the strongest move because it forces the dome to pick one event; or (b) revise Part 6.14 to match WIN-012 (2003 Halloween storm) — but this conflicts with the dome's live-power source label and weakens the eclipse-test framing. Option (a) is preferred. After settling, propagate to the TLDR (Hole 1).

holes_found.description: Missing cross-reference to WIN-070. WIN-012's compressed Cross-references paragraph points to WIN-011, WIN-013, WIN-014, and Part 6.14. It does NOT reference WIN-070 (claim: 'Forensic Zoom chart proves kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal via L1A induction-residual analysis,' verdict: Refuted by Data). WIN-070 is the new home for the GRACE Level-1A graph-fabrication critique that the pre-compression WIN-012 carried inline (uGal-on-L1A unit mismatch, GRACE orbital-mechanics impossibility for 27-minute co-temporal Boulder comparison, missing GRACE processing code in the dome repo, r=0.992 and AI=1.152 as static HTML). A reader who lands on WIN-012 and wants to evaluate the dome's prominent home-page proof of kappa has no pointer to that dedicated rebuttal.
holes_found.recommendation: Append one cross-reference to WIN-012's Cross-references paragraph: 'For the dome's home-page Forensic Zoom chart claiming to show kappa via GRACE L1A residuals (BOU magnetic + L1A induction r = 0.992, AI = 1.152), see WIN-070.' This closes the rebuttal-locator gap created by relocating the GRACE forensics out of WIN-012.

holes_found.description: Compression dropped the explicit kappa-arithmetic novel self-contradiction; preserved (with W004 baseline expansion) in Part 6.14. The pre-compression WIN-012 ran: at kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal, applying the dome's own forward magnetic dB = -17 to -21 nT yields dg = -10.2 to -12.6 uGal (verified: 17/1.6769 = 10.14, 21/1.6769 = 12.52) — consistent with PRED-TIER-3 but incompatible with the -6.5 uGal Mohe datum that defined kappa. Part 6.14 now develops this further (back-implied 16.8-21.8 nT premise, Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble bound 5-15 nT, W004 vs BOU baseline disambiguation). Acceptable consolidation, but a reader landing on WIN-012 alone loses the arithmetic punchline.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add one inline sentence to WIN-012's defender-rescues paragraph quoting the arithmetic ('Applying kappa to the 10-13 uGal target back-implies dB = 16.8 to 21.8 nT at the named stations — within the upper edge of the global ensemble (5-15 nT, Kim & Chang 2018, 207 measurements) only under the dome's W004 baseline (-22.24 nT), not the BOU baseline (-10.9 nT). See Part 6.14 for full closure.'). Low priority — Part 6.14 carries the load. Do not block on this.

holes_found.description: Compression dropped Yang & Wang 2002 citation; preserved in Part 6.14. The pre-compression WIN-012 cited Yang & Wang 2002 (Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245-253, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985) — the lead Mohe observer's own retreat from the shielding interpretation, with quoted text 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found' once additional measurement controls were applied. The compressed text retains the Unnikrishnan 2001 reference but loses the observer's own retreat. Rhetorically, Yang & Wang 2002 is stronger because it is the dome's own data source revising itself, not an external critic. Part 6.14 preserves the citation and quote in its 'How kappa was derived' subsection. Acceptable consolidation; flagged for awareness only.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add 'including the lead observer's own subsequent retraction in Yang & Wang (2002)' to the WIN-011 reference clause in the Three-step refutation step (1). Low priority.

holes_found.description: PRED-TIER-2 -> PRED-TIER-3 update is correct but unannotated. Pre-compression WIN-012 referenced PRED-TIER-2 (which is actually the retrodiction 'restates_win: WIN-011' per predictions.json). Compressed text references PRED-TIER-3 (the prospective Aug 2026 SG test). The update is correct — PRED-TIER-3 is the genuine forward prediction kappa owns. But a reader cross-checking predictions.json sees three TIER predictions and may wonder why only PRED-TIER-3 is invoked. One-clause annotation would help: 'PRED-TIER-3 (the prospective Aug 2026 SG test; PRED-TIER-2 is a restatement of WIN-011).' Very low priority.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-clause annotation as above. Skip if other compressions show this is not a reader-confusion pattern in practice.

stronger_arguments.description: The strongest single move is to surface the dome's OWN internal inconsistency about which event provides kappa's numerator. The live-power tile says 'BOU 2017 eclipse'; the home-page Forensic Zoom chart shows the 2003 Halloween storm (Oct 30, 00:55-01:22 UTC, Dst = -383 nT per WIN-070). The dome cannot have it both ways: either the -10.9 nT is the 2017 eclipse (in which case the storm-context r = 0.992 of WIN-058b is a separate, unconnected coupling that the dome has nonetheless labeled with the same kappa symbol — a category error), or the -10.9 nT is the 2003 storm (in which case kappa is a storm-magnetic / eclipse-gravity ratio, not a magnetic-gravity coupling at all — different physical regimes). Either reading destroys WIN-012 as a 'two independent eclipse events, same coupling constant' confirmation. Currently our review doesn't weaponize this — both WIN-012 and Part 6.14 just pick one event each and roll forward.
sources: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:224-226 (Domain EM-Gravity Coupling kappa: BOU 2017 eclipse)
data/wins.json[id=070] detail_evidence (Forensic Zoom chart labeled Oct 30, 2003, G5 Halloween storm)
data/wins.json[id=058b] (Bedrock Induction Ratio kappa from 2003 storm BOU & SG correlation)

stronger_arguments.description: Promote the kappa-as-bridge structural argument from Part 6.14 to WIN-012's headline finding. Currently WIN-012's 'finding' field reads: 'WIN-012 claims 6.5 uGal eclipse gravity anomaly to derive kappa = 1.67, but WIN-013/014 report 0.0 uGal — denominator vanishes, kappa undefined. Coupling constant derived circularly from unconfirmed Mohe data; monitor.py validates via hardcoded identity check.' This captures the surface contradiction but not the bridge. Stronger framing: 'kappa is the only published bridge between the dome's magnetic predictions (PRED-TIER-1) and its gravity predictions (PRED-TIER-3). Either kappa applies (in which case 1999/2009 SG nulls refute the model) or kappa has unpublished scope conditions (in which case PRED-TIER-3 has no derivation). The dome cannot drop kappa without dropping its strongest live discriminator.' This is the actual structural vise — and surfacing it in the finding field would make it visible to readers who never expand the detail.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model is making a genuine attempt at a unified physical claim: that eclipse-induced magnetic perturbations and eclipse-induced gravity perturbations are linked through a single coupling constant. This is the structure of real physics — universal constants linking conjugate observables (G, c, epsilon_0, etc.). If the dome had a derivation of kappa from disc_radius and firmament_height that yielded 1.677 nT/uGal independent of the Mohe and BOU observations, and that derivation predicted measurable signals at 1999 Membach and 2009 Chinese SGs that subsequently appeared, kappa would be a genuine prediction (data/wins.json[id=012].claim, data/sections.json:part6.html#p6-614-kappa-cluster 'What kappa is and why the dome cannot abandon it' subsection).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The unification ambition does not survive the empirical chain. (a) kappa is computed post-hoc from two pre-existing measurements (data/wins.json[id=012].finding, raw-text/02-wins.txt:87 'Source: BOU 2017 + Mohe 1997'); (b) the dome publishes no derivation of kappa from dome-geometric parameters (data/wins.json[id=012].code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false); (c) the only prospective test the kappa value enables — PRED-TIER-3's 10-13 uGal SG pulse at August 2026 contact phases — already failed prospectively at 1999 Membach and 2009 Chinese sites (data/wins.json[id=013].verdict, data/wins.json[id=014].verdict, data/sections.json:part6.html#p6-614-kappa-cluster 'What past SG data actually shows'). 'Universal constant linking conjugate observables' requires the constant to actually link; if it doesn't link 1999 and 2009 eclipses, the universality claim collapses to two unrelated measurements from one storm and one eclipse, with the same numeric ratio by curve-fitting.

deeper_analysis:
Post-EXP-401 the architecture of the kappa argument has shifted: WIN-012 now serves as a brief, high-level headline (526 words detail) while Part 6.14 carries the full structural-vise argument (~22,800 char ~ 3,500 words). This is sound consolidation — the kappa argument touches WIN-010 (numerator), WIN-011 (denominator quality), WIN-012 (definition), WIN-013 (1999 refutation), WIN-014 (2009 refutation), WIN-058b (storm corollary), and WIN-070 (graph forensics). Centralizing the structural vise into one cross-referenced section is cleaner than duplicating it in seven WIN entries. The compression is a net win.

The net cost is: (a) one TLDR factual error introduced and not caught (Hole 1, major); (b) one missing relocation pointer to WIN-070 (Hole 3, moderate); (c) one pre-existing cross-page provenance conflict preserved (Hole 2, moderate); (d) two minor losses of inline citation/arithmetic that survive in Part 6.14 (Holes 4-5). The major issue (Hole 1) is independent of the compression — the TLDR was written 2026-04-12 and has been live a month — but the compression cycle was a chance to catch it and didn't. Holes 2-3 are direct consequences of the consolidation and warrant patches.

Verdict: the kappa Coupling Cluster argument is rhetorically stronger after EXP-401, but the WIN-012 entry point itself has accrued a TLDR error and lost some load-bearing cross-references. Self-Contradicted verdict holds with high confidence (0.92) given the empirical refutation chain (WIN-013/014 nulls void the denominator OR the dome must publish scope conditions that exempt 1999/2009 in advance of August 2026).

One meta-observation for the decider: this is the second time in the audit trail that operator-direct integration (compression authorized 'without curmudgeon-in-the-loop') introduced a finding the curmudgeon would normally catch (the TLDR factual error survived because the operator wasn't running it through adversarial review). The PROP-041 sloppytoppy-rewrite pipeline added a content-preservation audit gate; EXP-401 was operator-direct compression and bypassed that gate by design. This is fine when the operator is the gate (as the commit message says: 'Operator (you) confirmed "much better" on the side-by-side comparison'), but the operator's eye is not the same as adversarial review. Consider whether operator-direct compressions should have a lightweight post-integration curmudgeon-verify hop (class=verification, 2-cycle SLA) to catch exactly this category of survivor. Not a blocker; just a process observation.

Yang & Wang 2002 DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985 — referenced in Part 6.14 (not WIN-012 detail post-compression); resolves to Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245-253 (Mohe reanalysis).
citations_unchecked: Sun et al. 2010 Phys. Earth Planet. Int. — referenced in WIN-012 detail as 'across a five-station Chinese SG network during the 2009 eclipse'; no DOI; WIN-058b notes the closest verified Chinese-language record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306 (2013), suggesting the 'Sun et al. 2010' label may be approximate. Not new to c7 — carried from c6.
Wang et al. 2000 Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101(R) — referenced in WIN-012 detail via the WIN-011 cross-reference; not directly resolved in this run.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender reads the compressed WIN-012 and constructs the following rebuttal: 'Your WIN-012 attacks kappa by saying WIN-013/014 nulls void the denominator. But the dome's WIN-013/-014 entries reframe those campaigns as not-tests of the current dielectric-boundary-transition prediction — the campaigns were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation, and the Chebychev low-pass filters contact-phase transients before Level-3 release. Your compressed WIN-012 acknowledges these escapes only as the defender-rescues-all-destroy-PRED-TIER-3 paragraph and gestures at Part 6.14. A reader who reads WIN-012 alone sees an attack-and-defender-escape with no inline closure. The dome's position survives at the WIN-012 entry-point.' The defender pushes further: 'Moreover, your TLDR says both from the same unreplicated 1997 experiment — but your own detail says BOU is 2003 Halloween storm and Part 6.14 says BOU is 2017 eclipse. Your TLDR is wrong, and your own review cannot agree on what event kappa's numerator comes from. If you cannot name the event, how can you attack the coupling?'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three preemptive moves close this defense: (1) fix the TLDR factual error (Hole 1) so the entry-point summary correctly attributes the two measurements; (2) settle the BOU-2017-eclipse vs BOU-2003-storm question (Hole 2) and update WIN-012 detail and Part 6.14 to agree; (3) add a single inline closure sentence to WIN-012's defender-rescues paragraph: 'These escapes require kappa to have unpublished, post-hoc scope conditions that exempt 1999 and 2009 while admitting August 2026 — a falsification-resistant carve-out (see Part 6.14 What the dome could do for the principled escape route).' Combined, these reduce defense_survives to <=1.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Cross-WIN inconsistency on kappa-numerator event provenance: WIN-012 detail_evidence labels BOU as 'October 2003 G5 Halloween storm, Dst = -383 nT.' Part 6.14 (sections.json:part6.html) labels BOU as '2017 North American eclipse, ~92% local coverage.' WIN-070 detail describes the dome's Forensic Zoom chart showing the 2003 Halloween storm timestamp (Oct 30, 00:55-01:22 UTC). WIN-058b uses the 2003 storm for its r = 0.992 correlation. The dome's raw-text/08-live-power.txt labels the kappa source as 'BOU 2017 eclipse' (live-power Domain EM-Gravity Coupling tile). Both the dome and our review carry the same internal inconsistency — neither has settled which BOU event provides the -10.9 nT that defines kappa's numerator. The dome's WIN-012 claim is 'two independent eclipse events' (raw-text/02-wins.txt:87), which is incompatible with the storm-context -10.9 nT but compatible with the 2017-eclipse -10.9 nT. Our review should pick one canonical attribution and use the dome's internal conflict (whichever one we don't pick contradicts the dome's own claim type) as additional ammunition — see Hole 2.

claims_checked: Forward chain PRED-TIER-1 dB -> kappa -> PRED-TIER-3 dg
1999 European eclipse predicted dg from published magnetics
quantitative_verification.computations: Python verification: 10.9 / 6.5 = 1.6769 (rounds to 1.68 at 2dp; dome quotes 1.67 — minor dome-internal rounding, not flagged). Forward: 17/1.6769 = 10.138 uGal; 21/1.6769 = 12.523 uGal — consistent with PRED-TIER-3 target 10-13 uGal. Back-implied: 10*1.6769 = 16.77 nT; 13*1.6769 = 21.80 nT — matches PRED-TIER-1 -17 to -21 nT band. 1999 dB = 5-15 nT (Strestik 2001, Hvozdara & Prigancova 2002 published magnetics) -> predicted dg = 2.98-8.94 uGal — 3-90x above 0.1-1 uGal post-reduction SG noise floor. Mohe self-consistency by construction: 10.9/1.6769 = 6.500 uGal = anchor datum. All arithmetic in WIN-012 detail and Part 6.14 verified to the resolution of significant figures available.

summary_for_decider: WIN-012 c7 review: compressed text (post-EXP-401, 2026-05-16) preserves the verdict (Self-Contradicted, confidence 0.92) but accrues 1 major + 2 moderate + 3 minor holes. PRIMARY MUST-FIX: TLDR factual error (Hole 1, major) — tldr_evidence claims numerator and denominator both come from the 1997 experiment; they don't. Live for a month, missed by c2-c6. Also patch the missing WIN-070 cross-reference (Hole 3, moderate) and settle the BOU-2017-eclipse vs BOU-2003-storm conflict (Hole 2, moderate, pre-existing pre-compression). The minor holes are optional Part-6.14-already-covers-it items; do not block on them. Verdict holds. Compression itself is a net architectural win — Part 6.14 now does the heavy lifting and WIN-012 is the entry point. Process observation: operator-direct compressions bypass curmudgeon-in-the-loop by design (per commit 66c9a741); consider a lightweight class=verification post-hop for the next operator-direct compression to catch survivors like Hole 1.
